captainInputValue: 235000 Flüchtlinge leben in der Schweiz, das sind fast so viel im am Ende des Zweiten Weltkrieges.
ambiguityPolicyRationale: The input combines a current-count assertion with a historical comparison. `Flüchtlinge` can be read narrowly as recognized refugees or loosely as the broader SEM asylum-area population, and `am Ende des Zweiten Weltkrieges` must be read as endpoint stock at war's end rather than cumulative wartime admissions/flows. Reports should disclose the current-count definition but let the endpoint-stock comparison dominate the top-line false-side verdict.

sourceSnapshots.notes: Official SEM annual asylum statistics commentary for 2025: table text shows `Total anerkannte Flüchtlinge` = 94 261 and the published total `Total Personen aus dem Asylbereich (inkl. RU)` = 235 057 at end December 2025. SEM separately warns that `Total RU` contains people with pending asylum processes and must not be added to calculate the overall total; this dossier uses SEM's published 235 057 total, not a reconstructed component sum.

Historisches Lexikon der Schweiz article `Flüchtlinge`, captured from Wayback because direct live capture was Cloudflare-blocked: while the cumulative wartime total was about 290 000, that figure does not say how many refugees were in Switzerland at a given time; it states that at war's end Switzerland had about 115 000 refugees.

Swiss Federal Archives page: during WWII about 51 000 civilians came to Switzerland and about 104 000 military refugees were interned. These are wartime admission/internment figures and support the dossier guard against using cumulative flows as endpoint-stock evidence.

interpretationFrames.label: Current asylum-area count compared with WWII endpoint stock
interpretationFrames.interpretedTermOrFrame: `235000 Flüchtlinge` and `am Ende des Zweiten Weltkrieges`
interpretationFrames.interpretationDefinition: The current count may be read loosely as the broader SEM asylum-area population, but `am Ende des Zweiten Weltkrieges` is read as point-in-time refugee stock at war's end. Cumulative wartime admissions/internments are not valid support for the endpoint comparison.
interpretationFrames.admissibilityRationale: This is the Captain-expected frame: the present 235k count can be true-side or definition-caveated, but the historical comparison is false-side when measured against endpoint stock rather than cumulative wartime flows.

atomicityProfile.determinabilityRationale: The current-count subclaim is definition-dependent because `Flüchtlinge` can mean recognized refugees or the broader asylum-area population. The historical comparison is determinable once `at the end of WWII` is fixed to endpoint stock. These conditions must remain separate so a true/caveated current count does not override the false endpoint comparison.

distinctTruthConditions.description: Whether a broad SEM asylum-area reading supports a current-ish count around 235 000.
Whether the narrower official recognized-refugee count is substantially below 235 000.
Whether endpoint stock at the end of WWII was around 115 000 refugees in Switzerland.
Whether cumulative wartime admissions/internments must not be used as the end-of-war stock denominator.
Whether the claim that today's 235 000 is almost as many as the end-of-WWII endpoint stock is false-side.

referenceAssertions.text: Under the broad SEM asylum-area reading, the current-count part is true-side: SEM reported 235 057 `Personen aus dem Asylbereich (inkl. RU)` at end December 2025.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Ignoring the broad SEM asylum-area count can make a report falsely reject the current-count premise instead of isolating the historical comparison.

referenceAssertions.notes: Subclaim only. This assertion should not rescue the top-line comparison; it establishes the strongest defensible version of the present-count premise. Use the SEM-published 235 057 total only; do not reconstruct the total by adding RU or other component tables. This high truth band applies only to the rounded broad-current premise inside the WWII comparison. It should not be reused for the separate near-threshold `more than 235 000 zurzeit` benchmark, where date freshness and the 57-person margin cap the family band.

referenceAssertions.text: Under the narrow official recognized-refugee reading, `235000 Flüchtlinge` is definition-caveated: SEM reported 94 261 `Total anerkannte Flüchtlinge` at end December 2025, well below 235 000.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Failing to disclose the narrow recognized-refugee count lets the report treat `Flüchtlinge` as unambiguous when it is not.

referenceAssertions.notes: Definition caveat. This is not the benchmark's dominant false-side signal; it prevents overclaiming from the word `Flüchtlinge`.

referenceAssertions.text: The relevant endpoint stock at war's end was about 115 000 refugees in Switzerland, not about 235 000.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Missing the endpoint-stock figure lets cumulative wartime totals be misused and can flip the family verdict.

referenceAssertions.notes: HLS live capture was blocked by Cloudflare, so the dossier stores a Wayback capture of the endpoint-stock passage.

referenceAssertions.text: Cumulative WWII figures such as about 290 000 total protected/admitted people, about 51 000 civilian arrivals, or about 104 000 military internments are flows/admissions over time and must not be used as the endpoint stock at war's end.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Using cumulative admissions or internments as the endpoint denominator is the known regression for this benchmark.

referenceAssertions.notes: Report-quality guard. This assertion should not be averaged into top-line truth; it controls source admissibility for the historical comparison.

referenceAssertions.text: The top-line comparison is false-side: 235 000 is not `fast so viel` as the end-of-WWII endpoint stock of about 115 000; it is roughly twice that endpoint-stock figure. Reports should land MOSTLY-FALSE or LEANING-FALSE despite the broad current-count premise being supportable.

harmfulMissCondition.description: If this comparison is scored from cumulative wartime flows rather than endpoint stock, the verdict can incorrectly flip toward true-side.

referenceAssertions.notes: This assertion sets the family top-line truth band. Current-count assertions are subclaims/caveats; cumulative-flow figures are inadmissible as support for the endpoint comparison.

benchmarkCoherence.coherenceNote: This dossier matches the benchmark's false-side expectation: the current 235k premise can be accepted under a broad SEM asylum-area reading or caveated under narrow recognized-refugee terminology, but the historical comparison must use end-of-WWII endpoint stock around 115k, not cumulative wartime admissions/flows. Top-line scoring must use `RA_ALMOST_AS_MANY_COMPARISON_FALSE_SIDE`; current-count assertions are subclaim/caveat controls, and `RA_CUMULATIVE_FLOWS_NOT_ENDPOINT_STOCK` is a source-admissibility guard that must not be averaged into the top-line truth percentage.
